revision.comment: 新規 茨木の式内社
revision.text:
==春日神社== {{神社| 名称=春日神社| 画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px|鳥居]]| 所在地=大阪府茨木市清水12-2-10| 位置=| 祭神=天児屋根命
武甕槌命
経津主命
比売大神| 社格=延喜式内社| 創建=| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月16日]]}} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は大阪府[[茨木市]]にある神社。[[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]。 ===歴史=== 式内須久久神社二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に天平12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣中臣清麻呂が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 元宿久庄村の属邑であったが、明暦元年（1655）に独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。明治5年に村社に列す。
===祭神=== 現在 *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] の4柱を祀る。
[[File:Simizu 003.JPG|250px|拝殿]] ===出典=== *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）
{{デフォルトソート:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:茨木市]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: [[春日神社（茨木市清水）]]を[[春日神社 (茨木市清水)]]へ移動: [[WP:NC]]

比売大神 |社格=延喜式内社 |創建= |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]]}} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の式内社の[[論社]]である。 ==歴史== 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、右大臣中臣清麻呂が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 ==祭神== [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] ==出典== *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かすかしんしや いはらきししみす}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:茨木市の建築物]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

link|Q11513674}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|version=107243065|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府茨木市宿久庄', 'language': 'ja'} ==References== [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] {{commons category|Category:Kasuga-jinja (Shimizu, Ibaraki, Osaka)}} [[JA:春日神社 (茨木市清水)]] [[ZH:春日神社 (茨木市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11513674}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|version=107243065|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|version=107243065|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府茨木市宿久庄', 'language': 'ja'} ==References== [[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] {{commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Shimizu, Ibaraki, Osaka)}} [[JA:春日神社 (茨木市清水)]] [[ZH:春日神社 (茨木市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11513674}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|version=107243065|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[比売大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 == 歴史 == 式内[[須久久神社]]二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]）4月5日、[[右大臣]][[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * [[明暦]]元年（[[1655年]]）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、[[春日神社]]と称した。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[村社]]に列す。 * [[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（[[2012年]]）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]] *[[経津主命]] *[[比売神|比売大神]] == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

[[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|version=107243065|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|天児屋根命}}
{{jalink|武甕槌命}}
{{jalink|経津主命}}
{{jalink|比売大神}} |社格={{jalink|延喜式内社}} |創建={{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。 == 歴史 == 式内{{jalink|須久久神社|Q11664487}}二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}）4月5日、{{jalink|右大臣|Q1351174}}{{jalink|大中臣清麻呂|Q11431854|中臣清麻呂}}が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * {{jalink|明暦|Q1192766}}元年（{{jalink|1655年|Q6975}}）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、{{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}}と称した。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に{{jalink|村社}}に列す。 * {{jalink|平成|Q234510}}22年（{{jalink|2010年|Q1995}}）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（{{jalink|2012年|Q1990}}）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 == 祭神 == [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *{{jalink|天児屋根命}} *{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}} *{{jalink|経津主命}} *{{jalink|比売神|Q22070227|比売大神}} == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Kasuga Shrine'''|春日神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[File:Simizu 001.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}清水2丁目10-1 |位置={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|天児屋根命}}
{{jalink|武甕槌命}}
{{jalink|経津主命}}
{{jalink|比売大神}} |社格={{jalink|延喜式内社}} |創建={{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。 === 歴史 === 式内{{jalink|須久久神社|Q11664487}}二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}）4月5日、{{jalink|右大臣|Q1351174}}{{jalink|大中臣清麻呂|Q11431854|中臣清麻呂}}が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * {{jalink|明暦|Q1192766}}元年（{{jalink|1655年|Q6975}}）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、{{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}}と称した。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列す。 * {{jalink|平成|Q234510}}22年（{{jalink|2010年|Q1995}}）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（{{jalink|2012年|Q1990}}）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 === 祭神 === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *{{jalink|天児屋根命}} *{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}} *{{jalink|経津主命}} *{{jalink|比売神|Q22070227|比売大神}} === 出典 === *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:かすかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{jalink|比売大神}} |社格={{jalink|延喜式内社}} |創建={{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}）4月5日 |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の{{jalink|式内社}}の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。 === 歴史 === 式内{{jalink|須久久神社|Q11664487}}二座の一つで、「神宮雑例集」に{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}）4月5日、{{jalink|右大臣|Q1351174}}{{jalink|大中臣清麻呂|Q11431854|中臣清麻呂}}が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったもの。 * {{jalink|明暦|Q1192766}}元年（{{jalink|1655年|Q6975}}）に元宿久庄村の属邑から独立し、{{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}}と称した。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列す。 * {{jalink|平成|Q234510}}22年（{{jalink|2010年|Q1995}}）8月28日 不審火による火災で本殿・拝殿を焼失。 * 平成24年（{{jalink|2012年|Q1990}}）3月25日 本殿・拝殿の再建が完成。 === 祭神 === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|焼失前の旧拝殿]] *{{jalink|天児屋根命}} *{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}} *{{jalink|経津主命}} *{{jalink|比売神|Q22070227|比売大神}} === 出典 === *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:かすかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{jalink|Hime-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}} |Founded={{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 12 (April 5, {{jalink|740|Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}-listed {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}} {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}} {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and is regarded as a {{jalink|ronsa|Q135022904}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{jalink|Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 12 ({{jalink|740|Q23593}}), {{jalink|Minister of the Right|Q1351174}} {{jalink|Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|Nakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{jalink|Meireki era|Q1192766}} 1 ({{jalink|1655|Q6975}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}}. * In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. * On August 28, {{jalink|Heisei era|Q234510}} 22 ({{jalink|2010|Q1995}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{jalink|2012|Q1990}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Himegami|Q22070227|Hime-Ōkami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{National Bibliography Number|89049621}} === Notes === on April 5 of {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 12 ({{jalink|740|Q23593}}), {{jalink|Minister of the Right|Q1351174}} {{jalink|Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|Nakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{jalink|Meireki era|Q1192766}} 1 ({{jalink|1655|Q6975}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}}. * In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. * On August 28, {{jalink|Heisei era|Q234510}} 22 ({{jalink|2010|Q1995}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{jalink|2012|Q1990}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Himegami|Q22070227|Hime-Ōkami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{National Bibliography Number|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasugajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] Village and came to be called {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}}. * In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. * On August 28, {{jalink|Heisei era|Q234510}} 22 ({{jalink|2010|Q1995}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{jalink|2012|Q1990}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Himegami|Q22070227|Hime-Ōkami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic {{jalink|Hime-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}} |Founded={{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 12 (April 5, {{jalink|740|Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}-listed {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}} {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}} {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and is regarded as a {{jalink|ronsa|Q135022904}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{jalink|Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 12 ({{jalink|740|Q23593}}), {{jalink|Minister of the Right|Q1351174}} {{jalink|Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|Nakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{jalink|Meireki era|Q1192766}} 1 ({{jalink|1655|Q6975}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}}. * In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. * On August 28, {{jalink|Heisei era|Q234510}} 22 ({{jalink|2010|Q1995}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{jalink|2012|Q1990}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Himegami|Q22070227|Hime-Ōkami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{National Bibliography Number|89049621}} === Notes === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (April 5, {{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (April 5, {{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=Himegami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Shimizu 2-chome 10-1 |Coordinates={{Coord|34|50|18.6|N|135|31|28.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (April 5, {{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=Himegami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{National Bibliography Number|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (April 5, {{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=Himegami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{National Bibliography Number|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (April 5, {{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (April 5, {{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (かすがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=Himegami}} === References === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Education) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{National Bibliography Number|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|WD=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|WD=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|WD=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|WD=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}-listed {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and is regarded as a {{ill|lt=ronsa|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} (shrine identified through scholarly debate). === History === One of the two seats of the Shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Sukuku Shrine|Q11664487|qid=Q11664487|1=Sukuku Shrine}}, according to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|qid=Q23593|1=740}}), {{ill|lt=Minister of the Right|Q1351174|qid=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro|Q11431854|qid=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi no Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deities near his residence. * In {{ill|lt=Meireki era|Q1192766|qid=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1655|Q6975|qid=Q6975|1=1655}}), it became independent from the affiliated villages of Moto-Sukuku-shō Village and came to be called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|qid=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|qid=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * called {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|qid=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * On August 28, {{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 ({{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|qid=Q1995|1=2010}}), a fire caused by suspected arson destroyed the main hall and worship hall. * On March 25, Heisei 24 ({{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}}), reconstruction of the main hall and worship hall was completed. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Simizu 003.JPG|thumb|right|Former worship hall before the fire]] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} *
